compare internal sales vs market sales i:
WITH Internal AS (
  SELECT ib.name, SUM(ms.product_quantity) as qty 
  FROM "master_sale" ms 
  JOIN "customer_details" cd ON ms."customer_id" = cd."customer_id" 
  JOIN "ims_brick" ib ON cd."ims_brick_id" = ib."id" 
  WHERE ms.area_name ILIKE '%F.B.AREA%' 
  GROUP BY 1
),
Market AS (
  SELECT ib.name, SUM(s."unit") as qty 
  FROM "ims_sale" s 
  JOIN "ims_brick" ib ON s."brickId" = ib."id" 
  WHERE ib.name ILIKE '%F.B.AREA%' 
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT 
  COALESCE(i.name, m.name) AS "Brick Name", 
  COALESCE(i.qty, 0) AS "Internal Units", 
  COALESCE(m.qty, 0) AS "Market Units"
FROM Internal i 
FULL OUTER JOIN Market m ON i.name = m.name
ORDER BY "Internal Units" DESC;
internal sales kuch bhi nhi ha kya:

FROM 
  master_sale ms
WHERE 
  ms.area_name ILIKE '%F.B.AREA%';
internal sales ka total data dikhow:
SELECT 
  SUM(ms.product_quantity) AS internal_sales
FROM 
  master_sale ms
WHERE 
  ms.area_name ILIKE '%F.B.AREA%';
internal sales ka data dikhow:
SELECT 
  product_name, 
  SUM(product_quantity) AS total_quantity, 
  SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue
FROM master_sale
GROUP BY product_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;
ye batow ke ye sales kis kis bricks or t:
SELECT 
  ib.name AS "Brick Name",
  SUM(ms.product_quantity) AS "Total Quantity",
  SUM(ms.total_amount) AS "Total Sales"
FROM master_sale ms
JOIN customer_details cd ON ms.customer_id = cd.customer_id
JOIN ims_brick ib ON cd.ims_brick_id = ib.id
GROUP BY ib.name
ORDER BY "Total Sales" DESC;
un managers ki list dikhow jin ke planne:
SELECT 
  m.name, 
  COUNT(dp.id) AS "Total Planned Visits"
FROM 
  managers m
JOIN 
  doctor_plan dp ON m.id = dp."managerId"
WHERE 
  dp.status = 'VISITED'
GROUP BY 
  m.name
ORDER BY 
  "Total Planned Visits" DESC;
waqas ke completed visit kis jagah pr hu:
SELECT 
  h.name, 
  dp."healthCentreId", 
  dp.status
FROM 
  doctor_plan dp
JOIN 
  healthcentres h ON dp."healthCentreId" = h.id
WHERE 
  dp.status = 'VISITED' 
  AND dp."managerId" IN (SELECT id FROM managers WHERE name ILIKE '%Waqas%');
managers ki completed visit ki detail ba:
SELECT 
  m."name", 
  COUNT(dp."id") AS "Completed Visits"
FROM 
  managers m
JOIN 
  doctor_plan dp ON m.id = dp."managerId"
WHERE 
  dp.status = 'VISITED'
GROUP BY 
  m."name"
ORDER BY 
  "Completed Visits" DESC;
inke doctor planning dikhow:
SELECT 
  dp."doctorId",
  d."name",
  dp."healthCentreId",
  hc."name" AS "Health Centre Name",
  dp."managerId",
  m."name" AS "Manager Name",
  dp."date",
  dp."eventType",
  dp."description",
  dp."shift",
  dp."latitude",
  dp."longitude",
  dp."inHealthCentre",
  dp."distance_in_meters",
  dp."visited_date",
  dp."status",
  dp."approval"
FROM 
  "doctor_plan" dp
JOIN 
  "doctors" d ON dp."doctorId" = d."id"
JOIN 
  "healthcentres" hc ON dp."healthCentreId" = hc."id"
JOIN 
  "managers" m ON dp."managerId" = m."id"
ORDER BY 
  dp."date" DESC;
compare internal sales vs market sales:
WITH Internal AS (
  SELECT ib.name, SUM(ms.product_quantity) as qty 
  FROM master_sale ms 
  JOIN customer_details cd ON ms.customer_id = cd.customer_id 
  JOIN ims_brick ib ON cd.ims_brick_id = ib.id 
  GROUP BY 1
),
Market AS (
  SELECT ib.name, SUM(s.unit) as qty 
  FROM ims_sale s 
  JOIN ims_brick ib ON s."brickId" = ib.id 
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT 
  COALESCE(i.name, m.name) AS "Brick Name", 
  COALESCE(i.qty, 0) AS "Internal Units", 
  COALESCE(m.qty, 0) AS "IMS Market Units"
FROM Internal i 
FULL OUTER JOIN Market m ON i.name = m.name
ORDER BY "Internal Units" DESC
LIMIT 5;
which brick has the highest internal uni:
WITH Internal AS (
  SELECT ib.name, SUM(ms.product_quantity) as qty 
  FROM master_sale ms 
  JOIN customer_details cd ON ms.customer_id = cd.customer_id 
  JOIN ims_brick ib ON cd.ims_brick_id = ib.id 
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT name, qty 
FROM Internal 
ORDER BY qty DESC 
LIMIT 1;

show me total sales of last month:
SELECT 
  SUM(total_amount) AS total_sales
FROM master_sale 
WHERE EXTRACT(MONTH FROM invoice_date) = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE);
jis last se last month ke sale ha tu wo:
SELECT 
  SUM(total_amount) AS total_sales
FROM master_sale 
WHERE EXTRACT(MONTH FROM invoice_date) = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE);
show me the sale of last quarter:
SELECT 
  SUM(total_amount) AS total_sales
FROM master_sale 
WHERE EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM invoice_date) = EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) = CASE 
    WHEN EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM CURRENT_DATE) = 1 THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1 
    ELSE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) 
  END;
which brick has a highest sale:
SELECT 
  ib.name AS "Brick Name", 
  SUM(is_.unit) AS "Total Sales"
FROM 
  ims_sale is_ 
  JOIN ims_brick ib ON is_."brickId" = ib.id
GROUP BY 
  ib.name
ORDER BY 
  "Total Sales" DESC
LIMIT 1;
which 3 products have the highest invoic:
SELECT product_name, SUM(product_quantity) as total_qty
FROM master_sale
GROUP BY product_name
ORDER BY total_qty DESC
LIMIT 3;
doctors ki age?:
SELECT 
  id, name
FROM 
  doctors
WHERE 
  id IN (SELECT id FROM doctors)
LIMIT 10;
list top 5 doctors by visit count in doc:
SELECT 
  d.name, 
  COUNT(dp."id") AS "Visit Count"
FROM 
  doctor_plan dp
JOIN 
  doctors d ON dp."doctorId" = d.id
GROUP BY 
  d.name
ORDER BY 
  "Visit Count" DESC
LIMIT 5;
show me top 3 invoice by quantity:
SELECT 
  id, 
  product_quantity
FROM 
  invoice_details
ORDER BY 
  product_quantity DESC
LIMIT 3;
by revenue:
SELECT 
  id, 
  product_id, 
  invoice_id, 
  product_quantity, 
  product_price, 
  product_quantity * product_price AS revenue
FROM 
  invoice_details
ORDER BY 
  revenue DESC
LIMIT 3;
